steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The skill is Command of Evidence (finding a quotation that best illustrates a claim). The claim is that Newland "visualizes the details of a future with May" — the correct quotation must show active mental imagination of an upcoming life with May, not present-moment observation at the opera.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — "[May] dropped her eyes to the immense bouquet of lilies-of-the-valley on her knee, and Newland Archer saw her white-gloved finger-tips touch the flowers softly.": describes Newland observing May's physical actions in the present moment at the opera. No future visualization is shown. ✗
  • Choice B — "Newland Archer, leaning against the wall at the back of the club box [where his seat was], turned his eyes from the stage and scanned the opposite side of the house.": describes Newland looking around the opera house in the present. He is observing, not imagining a future. ✗
  • Choice C — "Though there was already talk...of a new Opera House which should compete in costliness and splendour with those of the great European capitals, the world of fashion was still content to reassemble every winter in the shabby red and gold boxes of the sociable old Academy [of Music].": describes the opera house setting and society's attitude toward it. Newland is not mentioned and no future with May is visualized. ✗
  • Choice D — "Already [Newland's] imagination, leaping ahead of the engagement ring, the betrothal kiss and the [wedding] march from Lohengrin, pictured [May] at his side.": the words "imagination" and "pictured" directly show Newland mentally projecting forward to specific future milestones with May. ✓
That is the correct answer.
